w:r.w:t: Review the workshop creation app and define requirements for automation.
The app must auto-select the correct landing page template (e.g., "solo" vs. "duo") based on the number of coaches and the workshop category (e.g., "AI" vs. "Exit").
This is a hard requirement for launch. The app must embed a JS snippet on the thank-you page to track coach commissions, passing the workshop type and price as variables.
Replace the current inefficient process (a new Zapier sync per workshop) with a single, automated sync that pushes all new registrants to HubSpot.
The long-term goal is a one-click approval process for Suzanne, eliminating manual data entry and enabling scaling to 100+ workshops/month.
The current workflow is manual and inefficient, requiring the admin (Suzanne) to repeatedly select templates and re-enter coach information.
The app should auto-select the correct landing page template based on workshop details, eliminating manual steps.
Auto-populate from existing records; provide a "Create Bio" button for new coaches.
Determined by the presence of a "primary" vs. "primary + co-coach."
An admin screen is needed to map workshop categories to their corresponding templates, allowing for future expansion.
Replace "half-day/full-day" with explicit "start time" and "end time" fields for clarity on landing pages.
Remove the "Workshop Type" field, as its function is covered by "Workshop Category."
After publishing a page, the app must return to the workshop dashboard, not leave the user in a new tab.
The "View Landing Page" button on the workshop dashboard is broken and must be fixed.
The current HubSpot sync process is unsustainable. It requires creating a new Zapier integration for each workshop, which is not scalable.
The app must automatically push all new registrants to HubSpot.
Create or update a contact in HubSpot.
Sync registrant name, email, and workshop details.
Enable a single HubSpot contact record to show all workshops they've attended.
Tracking coach commissions requires a manual process of creating separate thank-you pages for each price point to hardcode the value for iDevAffiliate.
The app must embed a JavaScript snippet on the thank-you page to automatically track sales.
The snippet must pass the workshop type and price, eliminating the need for multiple pages.
This integration is mandatory before the app can go live.
The current "Requested" status for new workshops is too simple and doesn't support the required approval process.
Implement a clear approval process to ensure quality control and proper setup.
The approver (e.g., Suzanne) receives an email with workshop details and a link to approve/reject.
Approval triggers the auto-creation of all marketing assets (pages, emails).
An optional setting to notify coaches of new sign-ups is needed to allow them to reject attendees (e.g., other coaches).
Fix the post-publish flow to return to the dashboard.
Fix the broken "View Landing Page" link.
Draft a plan for landing page automation (solo/duo, category-based templates).
Draft a plan for HubSpot integration.
Schedule a meeting with Suzanne to define the approval workflow stages.
Get the correct iDevAffiliate JS code from Carolyn and send it to Joshua.
Get details from Carolyn on the specific fields synced from Kajabi to HubSpot.
Implement start/end time fields; remove max attendees for virtual workshops
Fix post-creation redirect to landing page editor
Add Primary/Co-coach fields; auto-select bio page; add Create Bio button
Implement coach signup notifications + registrant removal; add admin toggle
Send iDevAffiliate code to Josh via Slack; then Josh implements on thank-you page
Schedule + record call w/ Suzanne re: pricing/early bird required; send recording to Josh
Fix landing/registration/thank-you page navigation; return to workshop dashboard
Coordinate w/ Carolyn re: Kajabi→HubSpot Zap fields; then Josh implements HubSpot sync
Send Claude development workflow to Jeff
